He began his career as a child actor, portraying the young version of his father Anthony Perkins' character Norman Bates in Psycho II (1983), and later appeared in films such as Six Degrees of Separation (1993), Legally Blonde (2001), Not Another Teen Movie (2001), Star Trek (2009), and Nope (2022).
He was featured in the second episode of the Shudder documentary miniseries Queer for Fear: The History of Queer Horror (2022), in which he discussed the legacy of his father's role as Norman Bates in Psycho (1960) and its sequels.

At least 12 characters long"},{"url":"https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/new-longlegs-movie-nicolas-cage-1236564219/","title":"New Longlegs Movie in the Works from Nicolas Cage, Osgood Perkins","content":"And in a twist out of the Longlegs playbook, rather than original distributor Neon being involved, this one has Skydance-owned Paramount Pictures as the project’s new home.
Nicolas Cage is returning as star and producer.
Also producing are Brian Kavanaugh Jones of Range Media; Chris Ferguson, Perkins’ partner at their company Phobos; and Dave Caplan of C2, which financed the initial movie.
Longlegs earned $128 million worldwide on a budget of less than $10 million, becoming Neon’s highest-grossing domestic outing.
Lifshitz and Raphael Margules (Barbarian), and Walter Hamada’s 18Hz productions, with the latter behind the upcoming Passenger set for a May 22 release."},{"url":"https://www.indiewire.com/news/breaking-news/new-longlegs-movie-oz-perkins-nicolas-cage-paramount-1235189050/","title":"New 'Longlegs' Movie from Oz Perkins, Nicolas Cage Set at Paramount","content":"Plot details are being kept under wraps, but a source familiar with the project described it as a new film set in the “Longlegs” universe.
Perkins and Cage will both serve as producers, along with Brian Kavanaugh Jones, Chris Ferguson, and Dave Caplan.
He soon signed a multi-picture deal with the distributor and directed three more films in quick succession: 2025’s “The Monkey” and “Keeper” and the upcoming “The Young People,” which hits theaters later this year.
“There’s no question ‘Longlegs’ is still Perkins’ best film, but when the director delivered ‘The Monkey’ earlier this year Neon made a mess of the movie’s campaign,” Foreman wrote.
In addition to his directorial work, Perkins also serves as an executive producer on Kane Parson’s upcoming directorial debut “Backrooms,” which A24 will release on May 29.
Paramount has yet to announce a release date or production timeline for the new “Longlegs” movie."}]}

{"query":"upcoming Longlegs universe movie January 2028 release standalone sequel producers Brian Kavanaugh-Jones Chris Ferguson Dave Caplan","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.cinemaexpress.com/english/news/2026/May/06/longlegs-2-to-get-a-2028-release","title":"Longlegs 2 to get a 2028 release","content":"Title: Longlegs 2 to get a 2028 release
# Longlegs 2 to get a 2028 release. Longlegs opened to positive reviews upon release, Longlegs, made on a modest budget of $10 million, raked in $128 million. Nicolas Cage will return to play the feared serial killer Longlegs. Filmmaker Osgood Perkins' next ***Longlegs*** movie with Nicolas Cage, which comes from Paramount, will get its theatrical release on January 14, 2028, coinciding with the Martin Luther King weekend. The first film, which was released in 2024, features FBI agent Lee Harker, who establishes a connection between a string of disturbing murder-suicides after uncovering a pattern of cryptic occult messages tied to a shadowy figure known as Longlegs (Cage). She faces a deadlock in cracking the case as Longlegs doesn't physically commit the crimes but employs mental manipulation on his victims. Perkins, Cage, Brian Kavanaugh Jones, Chris Ferguson, and Dave Caplan are producing the film. Opening to positive reviews upon release, ***Longlegs***, made on a modest budget of $10 million, raked in $128 million worldwide. With his MGM+ superhero noir series ***Spider-Noir*** under post-production, Cage also has ***Madden, Spider-Man: Beyond the Spider-Verse, Lords of War, The Prince, Fortitude,*** and ***Best Pancakes in the County*** in the pipeline. ## Related Stories. ###### Nicolas Cage steps into shadows as 'Spider-Noir' trailer drops, unveiling gritty 1930s Marvel thriller. ###### Top Gun 3 in development; Tom Cruise to return along with producer Jerry Bruckheimer. ###### Osgood Perkins and Nicolas Cage to return for new Longlegs film. ###### Nicolas Cage's Lord of War sequel to get a 2027 release.","score":0.85567063,"raw_content":null,"domain":"cinemaexpress.com","popularity_score":3.654977902438255,"main_topic":"entertainment"},{"url":"https://www.hollywoodreporter.com?p=1236585688","title":"New 'Longlegs' Movie Set for 2028 Release Date","content":"Title: New 'Longlegs' Movie Set for 2028 Release Date
# New ‘Longlegs’ Movie Set for 2028 Release. Paramount is behind the project, which follows the 2024 Neon movie from Osgood Perkins. ### More Stories by Aaron. Paramount has set a return to the world of *[Longlegs*](https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/new-longlegs-movie-nicolas-cage-1236564219/). The feature will bow on Jan. 14, 2028, the studio revealed Monday. Filmmaker Osgood Perkins is back to write and direct the new movie, with star Nicolas Cage returning. The film is not exactly a sequel, but described as something in the *Longlegs* universe. Released in July 2024, *Longlegs* established Perkins as a name brand horror filmmaker, with the project grossing $128 million worldwide on a budget of less than $10 million. Neon released the first movie, with its marketing campaign cited as a blueprint for how to build anticipation for a spooky movie. ### Popular on THR. ### Box Office Haute Couture: 'Devil Wears Prada 2' Officially Kicks Off Summer With Heavenly $77M in U.S., $234M Globally](https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/box-office-devil-wears-prada-2-opening-1236582365/). ### Box Office: 'The Devil Wears Prada 2' Struts With Trend-Setting $10M in Previews](https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/box-office-the-devil-wears-prada-2-1236581489/). ### Box Office Haute Couture: 'Devil Wears Prada 2' Officially Kicks Off Summer With Heavenly $77M in U.S., $234M Globally. ### Box Office: 'The Devil Wears Prada 2' Struts With Trend-Setting $10M in Previews. That a new *Longlegs* movie would exist was a surprise when *The Hollywood Reporter* revealed the news last month. Also a surprise: that the franchise would move from Neon to Paramount. Sources previously told *THR* the indie outfit stepped aside as the project’s budget was above its norm, and Paramount stepped in, as horror is a priority at the studio. Perkins has stayed in business with Neon elsewhere, where he has a first-look deal and directed *The Monkey*, *Keeper* and his upcoming movie *The Young People*, which is expected in theaters this year and stars Nicole Kidman, Lola Tung and Nico Parker. Producers on the new *Longlegs* movie include Brian Kavanaugh Jones, Perkins, Chris Ferguson, Dave Caplan and Cage. ## THR Newsletters. Sign up for THR news straight to your inbox every day. Osgood Perkins‘ next *Longlegs* movie with Nicolas Cage, which was won by Paramount, will be hitting theaters on January14, 2028, the studio said Monday. By the way, that’s MLK weekend. The only other movie currently on that early 2028 date is an untitled release from Disney. The plot of the next *Longlegs* movie is under wraps. Producers on the project are Brian Kavanaugh Jones, Perkins, Chris Ferguson, Dave Caplan and Cage. Paramount has minted moolah from the four-day MLK weekend recently with the openings of 2022’s *Scream* ($33.8 million) and 2024’s *Mean Girls* ($33.6M). The studio’s record MLK weekend opening belongs to 2008’s *Cloverfield* ($46.1M). ### Watch on Deadline. The first Longlegs movie, released by Neon in July 2024, opened to the distrib’s best domestic debut weekend ever with $22.4M and legged out to cumes of $74.3M domestic and $127.9M worldwide. # A New ‘Longlegs’ Movie From Osgood Perkins And Nic Cage Is On The Way… But Not From NEON. Posted by Hunter Bolding | Apr 14, 2026 | Horror, Movie News | 0  |. A New ‘Longlegs’ Movie From Osgood Perkins And Nic Cage Is On The Way… But Not From NEON. NEON, the original distributor for *Longlegs*, the horror sensation of 2024, is not on board for a new movie in that universe. Instead, Osgood Perkins and Nic Cage will take their talents to Paramount for the new mystery *Longlegs* project. The Hollywood Reporter was first with the news about the sequel/expansion of the highest-grossing independent film of the past decade. Osgood Perkins is attached to write, direct, and produce the film with Nicolas Cage on board to star and produce as well. Also on board as producers are Brian Kavanaugh Jones of Range Media, Chris Ferguson of Phobos, and Dave Caplan of C2, who financed the original movie. Although this project is incredibly secretive, this is not a sequel to *Longlegs*, but another movie from that universe. The original film starred Maika Monroe and Nicolas Cage. NEON apparently passed on this new film because the budget went past their scope and normal parameters. Paramount has been building out a nice range of horror movies in recent years and they’re banking on this being another in a long line of hits like *Scream (2022), Scream VI, Scream 7, Smile,* and their upcoming *Paranormal Activity* sequel. ![A Complete Unknown: The Tims They Are A-Changin’ In A Stunning Transformation [Review]](https://www.thathashtagshow.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/3fde8ca3-a6c1-4412-b1d1-cdb51f390118-440x264.jpg). #### [A Complete Unknown: The Tims They Are A-Changin’ In A Stunning Transformation [Review]](https://www.thathashtagshow.com/latest-news/a-complete-unknown-the-tims-they-are-a-changin-in-a-stunning-transformation-review/). ## Cookie Consent.
